system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:

vertices

edges

);

);

mergePatchPairs
(

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     shallowWaterFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         100;

deltaT          0.1;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   1;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phiCorr,hU) Gauss linear;
    div(phi,hU)     Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    h
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-12;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    hFinal
    {
        $h;
        relTol          0;
    }

    hU
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-12;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    hUFinal
    {
        $hU;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    3;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='setFieldsDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

defaultFieldValues
(
    volScalarFieldValue h 0.01
    volVectorFieldValue hU (0.001 0 0)
);

regions
(
    boxToCell
    {
        box (0.4 0.4 -1) (0.6 0.6 1);
        fieldValues
        (
            volScalarFieldValue h0 0.01
        );
    }
);

<review_analysis># Error Diagnosis

## Error Analysis

The error is:

```
wrong token type - expected word, found on line 10 the doubleScalar 9.81
```

This is coming from `constant/gravitationalProperties` at line 10. The current file is:

```
g               9.81;
Omega           OmegaOmega [ 0 0 -1 0 0 0 0 ] 0;
```

The error says it expected a **word** but found a **doubleScalar** (the number 9.81). This means the solver is trying to read `g` as a **keyword/word**, not as a numeric value. In other words, `shallowWaterFoam` expects to see a literal keyword like `g` followed by its value structure, rather than just the bare numeric value.

Looking at the actual squareBump reference case's `gravitationalProperties`, the format is:

```
g               g [ 0 1 -2 0 0 0 0 ] 9.81;
Omega           OmegaOmega [ 0 0 -1 0 0 0 0 ] 0;
```

The solver reads `g` as a keyword, then expects to parse the associated value (which can be a dimensioned vector or scalar). When we removed the leading `g`, it started reading `9.81` directly as the thing to assign to `g`, but since it was expecting a keyword (word) to associate with the value, it failed.

## Proposed Fix

Omega           OmegaOmega [ 0 0 -1 0 0 0 0 ] 0;

<user_requirement>do a shallow water simulation over a square bump using the shallowWaterFoam solver. The domain spans 1 x 1 x 0.1, discretized into 20 x 20 x 1 grid points. The simulation initializes with a uniform water depth of 0.01 m and a uniform momentum field of (0.001,0,0). The simulation runs from 0 s to 100 s with a timestep of 0.1 s and writes results every 1 s. </user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/gravitationalProperties', changes='Change g entry from g 9.81 to g g [ 0 1 -2 0 0 0 0 ] 9.81; Keep Omega entry unchanged')]
